description: Machine-readable Agent Delivery Accuracy Matrix v1 contract for the agent-delivery-harness (issue #158). Companion authority for agent-delivery-accuracy-matrix.md: it defines the layered metric inventory, explicit denominators, absence semantics, blocking posture, and anti-Goodhart guardrails used to evaluate whether agent delivery is correct before it is efficient.

notes.anti_goodhart: The matrix is quality-gated: lower cost, shorter time, fewer tool calls, or higher merge rate can never offset correctness, review, security, trace, or lifecycle regressions. Metrics must carry explicit denominators and anti-gaming guards so agents cannot improve a number by skipping sensors, suppressing traces, narrowing coverage, or avoiding review.
notes.absence_semantics: null means no data; 0 means measured zero; [] means the detector/collector ran and found nothing. Missing fields are not coerced to zero. Rates carry explicit denominators, and coverage fields say whether a denominator is complete enough to compare across runs.
notes.accuracy_vs_merge: Merged means delivery COMPLETED, not delivery CORRECT. Accuracy labels come from acceptance criteria, review verdicts, regression/e2e sensors, post-merge defects, and trace evidence; GitHub merge state is only a delivery boundary and must not be used as a correctness label by itself.
notes.deferred_metrics: Deferred metrics are labeled honestly and never fabricated. Review-blocking findings per issue and per-feature tool-call attribution need additive trace-summary v1.x fields before they can be computed from trace summaries. Copilot-side token and cost capture is tracked separately in #163. Post-merge bug attribution requires a maintained post-merge issue/incident label source.
notes.open_world: Additive top-level keys and metric fields are allowed in later v1.x revisions; existing keys and semantics are frozen. Consumers reject unknown major versions of matrix_schema_version. New metrics must preserve explicit denominators, absence semantics, blocking policy, and Goodhart guards.
notes.attribution_window: trace-summary.v1 distinguishes finish from pr_merge: finished/final_outcome are finish-only, while bounded/closed_by may later identify pr_merge as a terminal close edge (#165). Accuracy metrics that judge agent work use the finish window unless they explicitly name a GitHub PR or post-merge source; merge/completion metrics use the PR merge window and are not correctness labels.

metrics.numerator: Issues whose final review verdict records spec compliance as pass against the issue acceptance criteria.
metrics.denominator: Issues with a completed final review verdict in the evaluation window.
metrics.source: review-verdict plus GitHub issue acceptance criteria
metrics.coverage_required: A final review verdict must exist for every evaluated issue.
metrics.absence_semantics: Missing review verdict is null/no label, not a failed or passed spec label; report coverage separately.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be raised by merging without review or by narrowing the issue acceptance criteria after implementation.

metrics.numerator: Pull requests approved by a human reviewer without a prior requested-changes or blocking-comment round.
metrics.denominator: Pull requests that received at least one human review decision before merge or closure.
metrics.source: github-pr review events
metrics.coverage_required: Human PR review data must be available for each included pull request.
metrics.absence_semantics: No human review means null/no approval label; it is not treated as first-pass approval.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be improved by bypassing human review or merging through automation without a recorded review.

metrics.numerator: Merged issues later linked to a confirmed post-merge bug, regression, revert, or incident attributable to that delivery.
metrics.denominator: Merged issues whose post-merge observation window has elapsed.
metrics.source: post-merge-issue labels, linked incidents, or revert records
metrics.coverage_required: Requires a maintained post-merge defect attribution source and a closed observation window.
metrics.absence_semantics: Missing post-merge triage data is null/no observation, not a measured zero defects.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be improved by shortening the observation window or failing to link regressions back to merged work.

metrics.numerator: Review findings marked blocking, major, or must-fix for an issue.
metrics.denominator: Reviewed issues with a structured final review verdict.
metrics.source: review-verdict (deferred: trace-summary v1 has no per-verdict finding field)
metrics.coverage_required: Requires structured review finding counts in a review verdict or additive trace-summary v1.x field.
metrics.absence_semantics: Missing finding counts are null/no data; they are not zero blocking findings.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be reduced by omitting review output, downgrading severities without rationale, or hiding review findings outside the structured verdict.

metrics.numerator: Feature-list items that reached a verified green handback or equivalent passing feature sensor result.
metrics.denominator: Feature-list items selected and attempted in the evaluated issues.
metrics.source: feature_list plus trace-summary final_outcome and lifecycle handback spans
metrics.coverage_required: Every evaluated issue must expose its feature inventory and final feature verification status.
metrics.absence_semantics: Missing feature status is null/uncovered; do not count it as failed or passed without a sensor result.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be raised by deleting features, merging unverified features, or counting implementation handbacks as verification.

metrics.numerator: Features whose first evaluator verdict was green without an earlier red handback for the same feature.
metrics.denominator: Features that received at least one evaluator verdict.
metrics.source: trace lifecycle handback spans; trace-summary red_reentry is only a related no-red-after-green signal
metrics.coverage_required: Requires ordered per-feature handback spans for each evaluated feature.
metrics.absence_semantics: If ordered handback spans are missing, the metric is null; red_reentry_free_rate must not be relabeled as first-pass green.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be improved by suppressing red handbacks, skipping evaluator runs, or relabeling reruns as first attempts.

metrics.numerator: Features whose declared regression/e2e sensors directly cover the acceptance criterion and were judged adequate.
metrics.denominator: Features with declared sensors in the feature list.
metrics.source: feature_list regression_sensor/e2e_sensor fields plus review-verdict sensor adequacy checks
metrics.coverage_required: Review verdict must assess sensor adequacy for each declared feature sensor.
metrics.absence_semantics: No adequacy review is null/no adequacy label; a skipped sensor is not adequate by default.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be raised by declaring trivial sensors, weakening assertions, or replacing e2e coverage with unrelated smoke checks.

metrics.numerator: Runs whose trace-summary has finished true and final_outcome equal to pass.
metrics.denominator: Runs aggregated in trace-scorecard by_version[].runs or trace-summary files in the evaluation window.
metrics.source: trace-summary finished/final_outcome and trace-scorecard by_version[].passed/runs
metrics.coverage_required: Each run must have a valid trace-summary v1 object or be reported as skipped/missing by the scorecard.
metrics.absence_semantics: Unfinished runs have final_outcome null; missing summaries are excluded from pass counts and listed as missing/skipped, never treated as pass.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be raised by emitting finish spans early, hiding failed runs, or excluding missing summaries from the coverage denominator.

metrics.numerator: Features whose verification evidence includes a red result before the first green result for that feature.
metrics.denominator: Features with ordered evaluator evidence in the trace or feature progress record.
metrics.source: trace lifecycle handback spans and feature progress evidence
metrics.coverage_required: Requires ordered evaluator evidence; trace-summary v1 red_reentry alone is insufficient because it detects red after green.
metrics.absence_semantics: Missing ordered evidence is null/no TDD evidence, not a measured absence of red-first proof.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be raised by fabricating a red run, weakening tests to fail trivially, or counting production errors unrelated to the target sensor.

metrics.numerator: Loop indicator groups by signature/type with count at or above the trace-summary threshold.
metrics.denominator: Runs with a valid trace-summary loop_indicators detector result.
metrics.source: trace-summary loop_indicators and trace-scorecard by_version[].issues[].loop_indicator_groups
metrics.coverage_required: Valid trace-summary v1 loop_indicators array for every included run.
metrics.absence_semantics: [] means the loop detector ran and found no repeated groups; missing loop_indicators is null/no detector output.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be reduced by changing volatile span fields, dropping repeated spans, or splitting one loop across artificial signatures.

metrics.numerator: Deviation lifecycle events counted across evaluated runs.
metrics.denominator: Runs aggregated in the same evaluation window.
metrics.source: trace-summary deviations {count, feature_ids} and trace-scorecard by_version[].deviations
metrics.coverage_required: Valid trace-summary deviations object for every included run.
metrics.absence_semantics: deviations.count 0 with feature_ids [] is a measured zero; missing deviations is null/no data.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be reduced by avoiding deviation logging when harness rules are bypassed.

metrics.numerator: Finished passing runs whose trace-summary red_reentry array is empty.
metrics.denominator: Runs in the bucket, reported as trace-scorecard by_version[].red_reentry_free_rate.of.
metrics.source: trace-summary red_reentry and trace-scorecard by_version[].red_reentry_free_rate {free, of}
metrics.coverage_required: Valid trace-summary red_reentry array and scorecard explicit of denominator.
metrics.absence_semantics: [] means no red-after-green re-entry was detected; missing red_reentry is null and must not be counted free.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be improved by hiding red handbacks after green or by relabeling the metric as first-pass green.

metrics.numerator: Detected violations of conductor/generator/evaluator/reviewer role boundaries, such as implementation touching tests or evaluator editing production.
metrics.denominator: Issues or feature phases with role-scoped handbacks/reviews in the evaluation window.
metrics.source: review-verdict, git diff file classes, and role handback logs
metrics.coverage_required: Requires role identity plus changed-file classification for each phase.
metrics.absence_semantics: Missing role or diff classification is null/no boundary audit, not zero violations.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be reduced by omitting handbacks, combining roles in one unclassified session, or moving tests outside tests/.

metrics.numerator: Runs with valid summaries, no invalid trace lines, required top-level summary fields present, and expected coverage fields for the instrumentation tier.
metrics.denominator: Runs discovered by the scorecard input scan, including missing and skipped summaries in the coverage report.
metrics.source: trace-scorecard inputs {summaries_found, missing_summaries, skipped} and trace-summary span_counts.invalid_lines/coverage
metrics.coverage_required: Scorecard input scan must include discovered, missing, and skipped summary counts.
metrics.absence_semantics: Missing or skipped summaries are coverage gaps; invalid_lines 0 is a measured zero only when span_counts is present.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be raised by ignoring unreadable traces, deleting failed trace files, or suppressing invalid-line counts.

metrics.numerator: Failed tool calls summed across runs.
metrics.denominator: Tool calls summed across the same runs.
metrics.source: trace-summary tools[] {calls, fail_calls} and trace-scorecard by_version[].tool_calls {calls, fail_calls}
metrics.coverage_required: Use trace-scorecard tool_coverage {runs_with_tool_spans, of} to separate instrumented runs from lifecycle-only runs.
metrics.absence_semantics: No tool spans with coverage false is null/no tool-call data, not zero failures; fail_calls 0 with calls > 0 is measured zero failures.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be improved by disabling tool instrumentation, avoiding necessary tools, or counting lifecycle-only runs as zero-call successes.

metrics.numerator: Repeated-loop groups, repeated failed sensor attempts, or repeated same-step handbacks indicating non-progressing work.
metrics.denominator: Runs with loop_indicators and ordered lifecycle handback data.
metrics.source: trace-summary loop_indicators plus lifecycle handback spans
metrics.coverage_required: Requires loop_indicators and ordered handback evidence for each included run.
metrics.absence_semantics: [] loop_indicators is measured no exact-repeat loops; missing handback ordering is null for handback-thrash subsignals.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be reduced by renaming repeated actions, dropping spans, or splitting one repeated attempt across multiple superficial steps.

metrics.numerator: Input plus output tokens for accepted issues when token data is captured by instrumentation.
metrics.denominator: Accepted issues with token coverage in trace-scorecard token_coverage.runs_with_tokens.
metrics.source: trace-summary tokens and trace-scorecard by_version[].tokens plus token_coverage; Copilot-side token/cost tracked in #163
metrics.coverage_required: Coverage-dependent: only comparable when token_coverage.runs_with_tokens equals the accepted-issue denominator or gaps are segmented.
metrics.absence_semantics: tokens null means token data unavailable; never average missing token runs as zero.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be optimized until correctness gates pass, and cannot be lowered by disabling token capture or excluding expensive accepted runs.

metrics.numerator: wall_clock.elapsed_seconds summed for accepted issues with wall-clock data.
metrics.denominator: Accepted issues with non-null trace-summary wall_clock.elapsed_seconds.
metrics.source: trace-summary wall_clock.elapsed_seconds and trace-scorecard by_version[].issues[].wall_clock_elapsed_seconds
metrics.coverage_required: Wall-clock data must be non-null for included accepted issues; missing timestamps are reported separately.
metrics.absence_semantics: wall_clock null means no timestamp data; do not treat it as zero seconds.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be improved by ending traces early, dropping timestamps, or accepting lower quality for faster completion.

metrics.numerator: Tool calls summed for accepted issues.
metrics.denominator: Accepted issues with tool span coverage, using tool_coverage.runs_with_tool_spans as the honest coverage denominator.
metrics.source: trace-summary tools[] and trace-scorecard by_version[].tool_calls plus tool_coverage
metrics.coverage_required: Coverage-dependent: compare only runs with tool spans or segment lifecycle-only runs.
metrics.absence_semantics: No tool coverage is null/uninstrumented, not zero tool calls; calls 0 is measured only when tool coverage is present.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be lowered by avoiding required tools, disabling adapters, or skipping validation steps.

metrics.numerator: Tool calls, handbacks, or lifecycle actions that directly advance an accepted feature according to per-feature attribution.
metrics.denominator: All attributed tool calls, handbacks, and lifecycle actions for accepted issues.
metrics.source: trace-summary tools[] and lifecycle spans (deferred for per-feature attribution; v1 tools[] has no feature dimension)
metrics.coverage_required: Requires additive trace-summary v1.x per-feature tool/action attribution before comparable computation.
metrics.absence_semantics: Without per-feature attribution the metric is null/deferred, never inferred from aggregate tool volume.
metrics.goodhart_guard: Cannot be raised by deleting exploratory evidence, hiding failed attempts, or counting only successful actions after the fact.
